rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target component or implementation scope, preserving AADL architecture
Place component type contracts in the component type scope, and implementation-level bindings in the implementation scope
Use visible identifiers from the current visible identifiers list when available
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use valid component type scopes for component type contracts and implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in contracts
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use AGREE logical operators such as `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>` in contracts
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence, for example `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
Branching logic should use nested `if ... then ... else`
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not use AADL property names as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement in component implementations
